title: Red Hawk Pantry
description:
1) Please have your Event Pass, Student ID, CWID and have a  PantrySoft Registration .  2) Please bring your own shopping bag! ***Visitors are allowed to come once/week*** 
 Our Mission:   We aim to address food insecurity across Montclair State University. The Red Hawk Pantry is open to all students, staff, and faculty at no cost.  Stay updated by following us on Instagram:  @montclair_redhawkpantry 
 🌻 Summer 2026 Hours 
 
 Wednesdays: 12-2pm 
 
 If you are unable to visit during our regular hours, please reach out via email at redhawkpantry@montclair.edu

title: Prestigious Scholarships Info Session
description: Join our weekly Prestigious Scholarships Info Sessions to learn about nationally competitive scholarships and fellowships that support graduate study, research, internships, public service, and international opportunities. Learn more about awards such as Marshall Rhodes, Truman, and more, while exploring eligibility, application timelines, and program support at  Montclair State University . Open to students from all majors. Sessions are held Wednesdays at 1:00 PM on Zoom. RSVP on Engage to receive the Zoom link. A google calendar invite will be sent once RSVP.

title: HABLAMOS ESPAÑOL - Spanish Conversation MeetUps - Summer Edition - 2026
organization: Center for Latino Heritage & Spanish Language Excellence
description:
Hola a todos y todas, 
 HABLAMOS ESPAÑOL is back for the Summer. Every Wednesday - starting May 20th, from 2-3pm - in person at Schmitt Hall 138. 
 No reservation required. 
 Just show up and come practice your Spanish conversation skills with Alondra. 
 Nos vemos pronto

title: ICO Dental Group – Virtual Info Session
organization: The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections
description:
🦷 Launch Your Career with ICO Dental Group – Info Session 
 📅  Thursday, May 28, 2026 🕛  12:00 PM – 1:00 PM 💻  Virtual (Zoom link provided upon registration) 
 Looking for a career where you can grow fast, earn, and make an impact—regardless of your major? Join  ICO Dental Group  for a virtual info session and learn about exciting  Sales Representative opportunities  with a rapidly expanding national company. 
 Headquartered in Princeton, NJ, ICO Dental Group serves  3,000+ clients across 40+ states  and operates offices nationwide. Their mission is to elevate U.S. dentistry by combining  innovative technology, strong local partnerships, and cost-effective solutions . 
 💼 Why Attend? 
 
 Learn about  paid internship roles  (open to all majors!) 
 Explore career growth in a  fast-growing, multi-state organization 
 Understand how ICO blends  technology, business, and healthcare 
 Get insider tips on the hiring process and what they look for in candidates 
 Ask questions directly to the ICO team 
 
 🌍 Who Should Attend? 
 
 Students from  any major  interested in business, sales, or client-facing roles 
 Students seeking  career growth and leadership opportunities 
 International students  — ICO offers  sponsorship opportunities 
 
 ✨ Whether you're exploring options or ready to launch your career, this is a great chance to connect with a company that’s growing nationwide and investing in early talent. 
 👉  Don’t miss out—register now and learn how you can get started with ICO Dental Group! 
 https://montclair.joinhandshake.com/events/1951243/share_preview

title: Library 101
description:
This workshop is your introduction to all the ways Montclair State University Libraries support your academic success. It covers what resources are available to you (e.g., books, articles, films, databases) and how you can access them, including through interlibrary loan. You'll learn how to navigate the library website, distinguish between popular and scholarly sources, and get help from a librarian when working on a research assignment. 
 Register Here:  https://montclair.libcal.com/event/16797394

title: 2026 National Leadership Conference (NLC)
description: This event is EXCLUSIVE to National Leadership Conference (NLC) qualifiers. Students with club membership CANNOT attend. Congratulations on qualifying for the NLC! Join us for our first National conference event as students represent Montclair State University on the National stage.

title: Safe Space Training
description:
Our Safe Space Training is an intensive, interactive training that provides attendees with information and tools to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, cissexism and heterosexism, on campus and beyond. Through interactive activities and discussion, participants will personally reflect on their own experiences to enhance their personal and professional development, provide further education, and finally, identify ways to create change on campus and beyond. 
 All sessions are three hours and are first-come, first-served. Pre-registration is required. Registrants are expected to attend the full session. Only individuals who have fully completed the Safe Space Program and have agreed to the expectations of its membership are able to display the sticker/button of our program. Refreshments are not provided; however, you are more than welcome to bring your lunch, coffee, snacks, etc.

title: Study Abroad Fair Fall 2026
description:
Ready to take your education global? Join us at the Study Abroad Fair and explore a world of opportunities waiting just beyond your campus. Discover programs around the world, meet representatives from our partners and providers, and hear firsthand stories from students who’ve already taken the leap. 
 Whether you're dreaming of practicing your language skills, expanding your horizons in a new corner of the globe, or even seeing a different part of the United States, this is your chance to find the perfect fit. Learn about scholarships, internships, and how to make your journey abroad both affordable and unforgettable. 
 📅 Date: Wednesday, September 9, 2026 📍 Location: Student Center Ballrooms ⏰ Time: 1:30-3:30pm

title: Fall 2026 Part-Time Job Fair
organization: The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections
description:
The Fall Part-time Job Fair offers students the opportunity to meet with both on-campus and off-campus employers interested in hiring various positions. The Fair will be held in the Student Center Ballrooms from 1-3 p.m., on Wednesday, September 16.  
 
 More Information : 
 
 Bring plenty of copies of your resume 
 Students must bring their Montclair State University Student IDs 
 Registration is required through Handshake 
 The location is the Student Center Ballrooms September 16, 2016 1:00 PM - 3:00 PM

title: Fall 2026 Career And Internship Fair
organization: The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections
description:
Looking to launch your career and gain experience through a full-time job or internship? The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections invites students of all levels to network with employers in a wide variety of industries! 
 More Information : 
 
 Open to all students, alumni, and community members 
 Bring plenty of copies of your resume 
 Students must bring their Montclair State University Student IDs 
 Registration is required through Handshake. 
 
 October 7 1:00 PM -  4:00 PM 
 UNIVERSITY HALL CONFERENCE CENTER

title: CSAM Internship (Co-op) Workshop!
organization: College of Science and Mathematics (CSAM) Career Services
description:
Workshop 
   
 ✨ Need to get into the internship course?  
 This self-paced session gives you step-by-step guidance about what you need to know and hwta the requirements are.  
 🔧 What You’ll Learn: 
 What are the requirements  
 What documents you will need 
 How to find an internship/job 
 Register on Handshake: 
 https://montclair.joinhandshake.com/events/1859416/share_preview
